_description: Phase-7 verification + hardening of the legacy `.claude/memory/` -> `.meowkit/` import for Codex-target flows. The import already existed (migrate.ts -> runMemoryPreflight -> runMemoryMigrationTransaction) with dry-run, quarantine, conflict hard-abort (exit 2), symlink safety, and an atomic staged/fenced transaction. This file records the dispositions decided while closing the remaining contract gaps (loss-aware, secret-scrubbed, rollback-capable, porter-independent).

dispositions.decision: Added a conservative text secret scan (memory-preflight/secret-scan.ts) run in buildEntry for text-classified files (binary cache class skipped). A hit QUARANTINES the file to memory/legacy/ (preserved, not published as a canonical store) and reports a count + types via the preflight report; the file is NEVER deleted or redacted. High-confidence provider patterns + one conservative credential-assignment rule keep false positives low; per the plan risk note, a false positive quarantines (no data loss) and the user resolves.

The transaction is roll-forward (resume), not roll-back, and pre-state is preserved by construction: legacy sources stay live until a fence-verified archive move; conflicts abort BEFORE any publish; a torn manifest fails closed (legacy authoritative). Covered by memory-migration-transaction.test.ts (crash-after-stage, crash-mid-publish, conflict-abort, torn-manifest). Added an explicit assertion that a conflict-abort leaves the legacy source live (pre-state intact).

init --target codex (and the multi-provider addCodexBundle path) copy the authored bundle only; they do NOT auto-run the legacy-memory import. Rather than silently skip, both now surface a hint pointing at `mewkit migrate codex` (loss-aware, conflict-safe). Import stays an explicit opt-in transaction so a codex-only install never silently mutates user memory.

Intentionally NOT implemented (YAGNI). The discovery walker reads the real `.claude/memory/` root; a misspelled sibling directory has never been observed in a real project. Revisit only if a real-world occurrence is shown.

The memory-import path (memory-preflight/*, migrate.ts memory step) has zero static imports from modules/porter/**. Guarded by a regression test that walks the import closure from the preflight entry points and asserts no resolved module lives under modules/porter/.
